question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `cfg.network` value of `'other'` combined with a `cfg.mirrorRest` URL pointing to an attacker-controlled HTTP server, causing `buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()` to fetch a poisoned node map and redirect all pinger traffic to attacker-chosen gRPC endpoints, resulting in the pinger being silently disconnected from the real network without any error surfaced to operators?
